questions.question: To add a member to your workspace using their email address, where should you navigate in your settings?

options.text: Settings → People → Members tab
options.explanation: According to the 'Add a member' section, you go to Settings → People or Members, then the Members tab, to add a member via email.

options.text: Settings → Security → General
options.explanation: Security settings are used for guest requests and workspace access, not for adding core members via email.

options.text: Settings → Domains → Allowed email domains
options.explanation: The Allowed email domains setting is used for automatic onboarding, not manual email invites.

options.text: Members → Invite link settings
options.explanation: Invite link settings are for sharing a link, not for typing in a specific email address to add a member.

questions.question: How can a workspace owner allow people to automatically join the workspace as members without manually adding their emails?

options.text: Enable the 'Invite link' toggle
options.explanation: The Invite link allows users to join automatically, but they are added as paid members without manual email entry. However, the question specifically asks about 'Allowed email domains' which is the other method described in the text for automatic joining based on work email.

options.text: Add their email domain to the 'Allowed email domains' field
options.explanation: Workspace owners can enter email domains in Settings → Settings → Allowed email domains. Users signing in with those domains will see an option to join automatically.

options.text: Turn off the 'Allow page access requests' toggle
options.explanation: This toggle controls whether non-members can request access to pages, not how new members automatically join the workspace.

options.text: Send a specific 'Join workspace' button link to the user
options.explanation: While a button is used, the method described for automatic joining via work email is the Allowed email domains setting.

questions.question: If your workspace has reached its guest limit for your plan, what happens when you share content with a new user who is not already a member?

options.text: The user is blocked from accessing the content entirely
options.explanation: The user is not blocked; instead, they are added as a member if they belong to an allowed email domain.

options.text: They are automatically added as a member if they have an allowed email domain
options.explanation: The article states that new users can only be added as members if they belong to the workspace's email domains when the guest limit is reached.

options.text: They are added as a guest, and you will be billed an extra fee
options.explanation: You cannot add guests beyond the limit, so billing is not affected by adding someone beyond the limit as a guest.

options.text: You must manually add their email address to the workspace first
options.explanation: The automatic domain check allows them to join automatically; you do not need to manually add their email address.

questions.question: How do you add a guest to an individual page within your workspace?

options.text: Go to Settings → People → Guests
options.explanation: Settings is used for workspace-wide management, not adding guests to a specific page.

options.text: Go to Share at the top right of the page
options.explanation: To share a page with a guest, you go to the Share menu at the top right of the specific page.

options.text: Navigate to the guest's profile in the sidebar
options.explanation: Guests do not have profiles in the sidebar; they are added via the Share menu on specific pages.

options.text: Send an invite via the 'Members' tab in settings
options.explanation: This would add them as a workspace member, not a guest to a page.

questions.question: On a Free or Plus plan, where can you disable the feature that allows non-members to request access to your workspace pages?

options.text: Settings → People → Members
options.explanation: People settings are for managing members and guests, not page access requests.

options.text: Settings → Security → Members & guests
options.explanation: You can toggle off 'Allow page access requests from non-members' in Settings → Security.

options.text: Settings → Settings → Allowed email domains
options.explanation: Allowed email domains control automatic joining, not page access requests.

options.text: Settings → Domain Management
options.explanation: Domain management is for verifying domains, not managing page access permissions.

questions.question: What is a key limitation regarding the approval of guest invite requests?

options.text: Guests can approve their own requests after submitting them
options.explanation: Guests do not approve their own requests; the request is sent to a workspace owner for approval.

options.text: Workspace owners can approve or reject requests from the notification or from Settings → Members → Guests
options.explanation: The article specifies that only workspace owners can view and approve guest requests, accessible either via the notification or Settings → Members → Guests.

options.text: Only Membership admins can approve requests
options.explanation: The text explicitly states that only workspace owners can view and approve guest requests.

options.text: Requests are automatically approved after 24 hours
options.explanation: There is no mention of automatic approval; the owner must actively respond.

questions.question: How does a workspace member request to invite a guest on an Enterprise plan?

options.text: They send an email to the workspace owner
options.explanation: Members do not email the owner directly; they use the page sharing interface.

options.text: They navigate to the page, select Share, type the guest's name, select a role, and Invite
options.explanation: The article details the specific steps for a member to request a guest: navigate to the page, Share, type the name, select a role, and Invite.

options.text: They must first ask the owner to enable guest requests in Security settings
options.explanation: The owner enables the general permission for members to request guests; once enabled, members follow the Share workflow.

options.text: They must create a public link and send it to the guest
options.explanation: Creating a public link is not the method described for requesting a guest invitation.

questions.question: What happens to a member's private pages when they are removed from a workspace?

options.text: They are permanently deleted from Notion's servers
options.explanation: The pages are not deleted; they are simply hidden from view in the user's left sidebar.

options.text: They are instantly hidden from view, but unhidden if the member is invited back
options.explanation: When removed, private pages are hidden. If invited back within 30 days, these private pages are restored.

options.text: They are converted into shared pages for the workspace
options.explanation: There is no mention of converting private pages to shared pages upon removal.

options.text: They remain visible to the member but are inaccessible to others
options.explanation: The member loses access to the workspace entirely, including their private pages.

questions.question: How can you upgrade a guest to a member so they have access to your entire workspace?

options.text: Go to Settings → People → Members → Guests and check the box next to the guest
options.explanation: You can find guests in Settings → People → Members → Guests, check their boxes, and select 'Upgrade guest(s) to member'.

options.text: Go to the guest's page and change their access level
options.explanation: Guests are added to pages, not to the workspace directly; you must use the Settings menu to upgrade them.

options.text: Invite them again as a new member using the email
options.explanation: You can invite them again, but the article specifically describes the 'Upgrade' feature in the People settings.

options.text: Turn off the 'Allow guests' setting in Security
options.explanation: Turning off the setting prevents guests from being added, not upgrading existing ones.

questions.question: Why might a workspace owner disable the 'Allow page access requests from non-members' setting?

options.text: To prevent members from inviting guests to pages
options.explanation: Disabling page access requests affects non-members, not members. Members can still invite guests directly or via request if enabled.

options.text: To reduce spam and control who can request access to workspace content
options.explanation: The article mentions this feature is turned on by default for all plans except Enterprise. Free/Plus plans can turn it off to control access.

options.text: To force all users to join as members automatically
options.explanation: This setting controls requests, not the method of joining (guest vs. member).

options.text: To allow guests to access private pages
options.explanation: Disabling this setting actually limits access requests, making it harder for guests to gain access.
